etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "to chirp")
etymology.interpretation: The character 喿(zào) visually combines three mouths (品) with a tree (木), symbolizing the sound of multiple birds chirping together in a tree. It serves as the original form for the word 噪(噪) (zào, "to chirp"), emphasizing the concept of noise made by birds.
